Well, I'm excited man. Thanks for having me on. And I'm really excited because I've taught a lot of these pieces in separate different places. And this is the first time I've ever kind of taught it all together. So it should be kind of fun. My screen looks like a mess right now because I have everything open you're going to learn about today. But then I'll start. I'll make it more organized here in a minute. So it should be kind of cool. All right. So we've got about two hours. That's what Joel gave me, which would be kind of fun to go through a bunch of stuff. And I'm going to be showing you guys first off what I call my perfect webinar. And I'll be breaking down the perfect webinar funnel. I'm going to show you guys the pages in our webinar funnels. I'm going to show you guys my actual webinar. Just it'll be fun. I got nothing to hide. So I'm going to show you guys anything and everything you want. If you have questions, then feel free to ask me throughout. I'll be looking at the questions here as well. But I'm just going to go in and we should have a lot of fun, you guys. So I'm excited for this. And excited for Joel putting this whole thing on. All right. So to begin with, so we're going to start over here with the perfect webinar. How many of you guys have ever seen this before? I'm going to make it big so you guys can see the whole screen. This is the focus of right now. All right. So this is the perfect webinar. And some back story on this. This is something that I didn't really invent it myself. It was initially, actually, let me step back even further. So when I first got in this business, I wanted to be an internet nerd. That's all I knew. That's all I understood. And I was online trying to sell things. It was really cool. And one day there was this guy who was one of my early mentors. And I really looked up to his name was Armand Moran. And he was talking about, he had this seminar at a time called The Big Seminar. And it was an internet marketing seminar where you can come and learn how to be an internet marketer. I'm like, well, I'm doing internet marketing. See how to grow it. Plus it'd be cool to actually meet other entrepreneurs. I'm not sure about you guys, but I always said being an entrepreneur is the loneliest job in the world. There's nobody that really gets you. Half the times your spouses don't understand what you're doing. Your friends don't. You're the weird guy. And so I didn't have any friends I could relate with. So I'm like, I can go somewhere. I think it was $2,000 for a ticket. I can go $2,000. I can go and spend this time and get no other entrepreneurs and just hang out. I was so excited. So I went to this event. And when we got there, there was, I think, there were about 500 people in the room. And I get there the first day. And I got my notepad out, my laptop. I'm so excited. And the first speaker gets up there. And he starts talking. And he speaks for about 90 minutes. And then when he's done, he tells everyone to go in the back of the room and to sign up for his program, which I think was like $2,000. And I was like, that was weird. But then all of a sudden I looked around. I saw all these people popping up all around the room, turning around and running to the back. And then throwing money at this guy. And I remember, I wasn't that good at math. But I looked back and I started doing the math. I was like, two, four, six, eight, 10, whatever. I was like, that guy just made $60,000. That was the craziest thing I've ever seen. And then next speaker gets up. And he's selling a $5,000 package. And he's an even better salesperson. He goes through the whole pitch. And at the end of it, everyone runs to the back and starts buying again. I'm doing five, 10, 15, 20, I'm like, that guy just made $100,000. Next speaker, the next speaker, for three days I watched this. And at the time, and some people don't believe me when I say this, but at the time I was a shy, awkward, nervous kid who just wanted to hide me on a computer. Didn't dare to talk in front of people. Didn't dare to, you know that stuff. But I was like, after seeing that over and over and over again, I'm like, I gotta learn how to do this. Cause that was amazing. I'd never seen someone make $100,000 in 90 minutes before. And I'm like, if that's possible, then I gotta figure out how to do it. So it kind of started my whole, my journey to kind of figure the stuff out. And back then it was before webinars were ever even thought of. We were using tele seminars initially. And so I started trying, you know, kind of testing, so like I'm gonna do a tele seminar cause I don't have to like talk to people or dress up. So I started these little tele seminars, my little list ahead of the time. And at first I'd go on a tele seminar, I'd just talk for an hour and then I'd try to sell people's stuff and sometimes it worked and sometimes it didn't. And it was cool because every week or so I'd do a tele seminar and I would just, I would try different things. I would try something weird and I'd try this and try that. And some things just didn't work and some things worked really good. And eventually I kind of started seeing like, okay, when I do things like this, people buy, when I do things like this, nobody buys, right? And again, I was never a good student, but like I liked money and I liked when I, you know, when something worked that I was gonna make money. I wanted to do that again. So I kept, I just, I started watching and noticing what was working. And about that time, this guy named Alex Mandozian, who's still just one of the most brilliant marketers I've ever met, he had this course called Tele Seminar Secrets. And remember it was $1,000 and they did this big whole product launch and they did it through tele seminars, funny enough. So I'm listening in day after day after day to all these tele seminars from everyone trying to help them sell this course. And I was like so nervous. I'm like, I know $1,000, that's a lot of money. Do I dare to do it? Do I not dare to do it? And finally one of my friends said, Russell, how many tele seminars have you done? And I was like, I don't know, like 15 or something. He's like, how much money do you make on all of them? I was like, you know, each one makes anywhere from, you know, a thousand to, you know, $8,000 or $9,000 or whatever it was at the time, I don't remember. And he said, if you made one or two extra sales from one tele seminar, from one idea you get from this course, like it would be like that pays your thousand bucks back and then they have everything, you know, from that point forward, everything else is free money. And I was like, you know what, you're right. Like it's a thousand bucks. Why am I freaking out about this? Like I sell people stuff all the time. Like if I'm gonna be a good, you know, good selling stuff, I gotta become good at purchasing things as well. So I put the, you know, I'm interested, I bought the thing and I went through his whole tele seminar course. And what was cool is it was the first time I'd ever thought about like, like the process. Like when someone's on the phone, what do you actually say? And in what order do you say it and how do you do it? You know, prior to that, I would just get on, I would just talk and I would teach and I would just, you know, whatever came to my head I would do. And he kind of started talking about like his script and like how he would do things. So I was like, oh, so I started kind of taking some of those ideas and implementing them into what I was doing for my presentation and on my tele seminars and I had some increased success. I was like, wow, these are cool. Like these are really good ideas. And he had ideas everywhere from, you know, how to get people on the tele seminar to closing on the tele seminar to what happened afterwards. Which a lot of, you know, a lot of what I do now is was inspired from that initially almost a decade ago. And so again, you guys will see a lot of that over the next two hours with some of the stuff I'll be sharing with you. But that was kind of my first time ever where I had step back, started thinking about this process. I remember doing one tele seminar with this guy named Mike Chen. And before the tele seminar, Mike got on with me to kind of prep me for the tele seminar. And I remember one thing powerfully taught me. He said, look, when I saw my product, he said, these are the three questions that everybody has. Number one was, you know, why does it cost so much? Why does it, whatever, he had the three questions. He said, what I need you to do is during this, during the tele seminar, I need you to ask me these questions in this, in this way. It's like you need to phrase them the exact same way I give them to you. And then the first question was like, hey Mike, so, you know, with all the features that are inside this software, I can't believe you're only selling it for $300. He said, you know, and why did you do that? And he had me ask the question that wakes me, knew that people's heads are like, well, 300 bucks is a lot. But then they hear me say, wow, everything that's in this, why are you only selling it for $300? Like, why is it so cheap? And suddenly it changes their perspective, right? And so he had me do that. And there's two other questions, I can't remember where they were now, but two other questions the same way that addressed his main concerns. And we did that and that tell a seminar, it was the first tell a seminar I ever had that broke five figures. I think it was like, I think it made $17,000 or $18,000 on it, which I was, for me, was just insane. Like that was more money than I ever dreamt of. And it came in one, back then it was a 60 minute call. And so I just became like, I was just like, this stuff is awesome. Little while later, one of my friends, Mike Phil, same as me, I know him, he invited me to come out to one of his events and come and speak on internet marketing. And so I'm like, sure, I've never done that before. Only event I'd ever been to was Arman's a couple months earlier, so I kind of seen that. And he said, when you come, I want you to sell something. I was like, oh, okay, cool, I can sell some stuff. And again, I've been doing these tell seminars, so I had a little bit of knowledge, but I still didn't really get it yet. And I remember thinking, when I was at Arman's event, I knew more about internet marketing than any of the guys who are up there speaking. I was like, I'm gonna go to this event and I'm gonna teach all my best, I'm just gonna blow their minds. I will give them so much value that they will run to the back room, and they're gonna give me money. Because I'm gonna out-teach everybody else. And that was my thought going to this thing. And so I spent weeks, weeks and weeks preparing this presentation, which is many, if you know me, that's not really my style. I'm a lot more, I'm a lot more like, just kind of off the seat of my pants, but I prepared and I prepared and I prepared. And I still think to this day, it was one of my best presentations ever. And it taught some of the most amazing things in internet marketing in a really simple format. And I did the presentation at the end of it. I tried to sell my product. Again, I put the word tried in there because I tried to sell my product and I did my little pitch at the end, just like I'd seen other speakers do from stage. And I did it and nothing, nobody moved. Like I could literally hear the crickets in the audience. And I'm looking out over this room about 200 people and I'm just like embarrassed and devastated and like freaked out. And I just done my pitch, it's pitch, you're completely quiet. No one's talking, there's no music playing, it's just kind of dead. And I'm done. I'm like, no, what do I do? Like how, like, I got to walk to the back of the room over there and I was like so scared. So like that's a long, it's a long walk away. I walked through all these people and it was like the walk of shame. So I'm walking back there all awkwardly and people starting to get up in their seats and people came back and started asking me questions. And a couple of things, one thing, people were like, that was the most amazing presentation I ever saw. Like, probably 20% of them told me that and then they walked away and they didn't buy it. None of them bought, none of them bought. And I was like, how in the world, like what I thought was so amazing, like it was everything they needed to be successful. Like why in the world are they not buying my thing? And I was so confused and I was embarrassed, honestly. So I shook hands, talked to a couple of people real quick and then I went to my hotel room. And I was so embarrassed, I literally did not leave my hotel room for the next three days. I locked myself in the room and I just watched cable TV, order room service and did not, I did not once leave the room the rest of the trip. I remember being so depressed and so embarrassed. I did not want to see anyone else at the event because I was so embarrassed. And so I'm hiding in my room and then I was like in my head, I was like, I will never, ever be a public speaker again. And that was so painful, so horrible. And I made $0 dollars out of it. I had to pay my own way here, pay my own hotel, I didn't make a penny, I lost money. Why would I ever do this again? And so I walked away from it. And I did for a while. And I kept doing tell seminars, which were working, I kept getting better and better at those. I started implementing some of these things we'll talk about here in a minute. Got better and better at it, but I didn't want to speak again. And a couple months later, I got another invite to speak at another event. I told them, no, they kept asking me and asking me and finally I was like, okay, I'm gonna come. I did the same thing, prepare an amazing presentation. I'm like, okay, this time, and I had kind of done like a basic, the way I'd kind of sold it at the end, the last time kind of bad. I was like, I'm gonna change how I do my pitch. I felt like last time I was insulting their intelligence. I was trying to do it like how the other speakers do it. And I'm like, that's not me. I'm just gonna be like straight up, straight up. So I go to the guys event, stand up on stage. And his room was a little smaller, maybe 100, 150 people. And I do my presentation at the end of it. These are my words. And I'm so looking back on it now, it's funny to me, but I said, I'm not gonna do what the other speakers do and insult your intelligence with talking about what this is worth and dropping the price. I'm just gonna tell you what the price is. It's $997. And you've gone through this trend, you know how valuable it is to me more of this and you need to meet me in the back of the room to sign up. And I ended and saved the crickets. I'm like, this is horrible. I have so much pain associated with this. I don't wanna do this again. And finally, I'm like, you know, but other people are doing it. Like I gotta figure out what in the world other people are doing to figure out how they're doing it. Cause it works, obviously it works. I'm not that dumb. I gotta be able to figure it out. Maybe there's something I can do. And so again, that was, this is, man, that's 10 years ago now. Seems crazy that that whole experience happened. And so by the time I started thinking, well, who is doing it again? I started looking around some of the people I was learning from and some of the mentors I had and stuff like that. And one of my top mentors was a guy named Dan Kennedy. And he, I went to one of his events and again, I saw the same thing. People run in the back and I was like, this is so cool. And so I went back to the back of the room. And they have all their courses they sell. And they had two different public speaking courses. One was Dan Kennedy's public speaking course and one was Bill Glazer. So I bought both of them. I'm like, I gotta figure this stuff out. So I'm listening to these courses and there's one like resounding theme I keep hearing throughout the whole thing with this with their courses. And the thing they kept saying over and over and over again, it says, look, you as a teacher, right? Like you want to go up there and teach and give and share. So the problem is you're doing that. You're doing your audience a huge disservice because there's no way in 90 minutes you can change someone's life. Like you can't give them the tools and the training and everything they need to be successful. It said your only job, your only responsibility during that 90 minutes is to inspire them and give them belief that what you are able to do will work for them. That's it. Okay, so I'm gonna repeat that. Your job in that 90 minutes is to inspire them and to give them belief that what it is that you are selling can help them. Okay, that's it. So if you can inspire them and if you can give them a belief that what you have will help them, they will give you money. That's it. Okay, you impressing them with how much you know, how good of a teacher you are will not. Well, first off, not gonna help them because they're gonna leave with an incomplete understanding to be able to implement what you showed them. And second off, they're not gonna give you money. Okay, and so we kept saying over and over and over again. I was like, that seems so backwards from logic. For me, it should be like, wherever we get the most value gets the most money, right? And that's true in parts of life, but not in a webinar or a stage pitch. When you're selling, it's different. And so I said, you know what, fine, I'm gonna just trust these guys. And so I started learning how they were doing their stuff and I watched their presentations and I modeled what they were doing. I went to my next event, had my new presentation where instead of teaching a whole bunch of stuff, I was trying to inspire them and give them belief that what I had to sell could help them and did my first pitch. And I still remember I sold six people the thousand bucks of pop, $6,000, which again, was a lot of money. I was just like, boom, like, okay, this is working now. Now I got a working model, how to make this better? What can I do and how can I make this better? And so I started going through a whole bunch of other people's public speaking trainings. And let me step back, not public speaking training because that's in my mind a bad thing. Like it's not public speaking, like how to be polished and clean, right? If you hear my webinars, you hear me even right now, like I stutter, I say things wrong, but man, I can get in front of a stage and I can close a room. Like I know how to do that now. It's not from speaking eloquently, it's from understanding the right way to give a presentation, okay? And so I started going there and I study every, I bought, man, I spent tens of thousands of dollars on different people's speaking courses. I found the best people, guys like Ted Thomas and I learned this whole concept called trial closing from Ted Thomas, which I'll probably talk about later on in the perfect webinar. So he taught me trial closes, added that to our webinars and we saw an increase where we were averaging $9 per webinar registrant, it jumped it to over $16 per registrant just by adding in trial closes. Not changing anything else to the pitch besides adding in trial closes. It went to, we'll blank out on their names, Ron the Grand, who's a real estate guy, went to his public speaking training and learned how he did it. Went, learned from so many people. Anyway, so I'm going through it. And I still remember the biggest thing I learned though and I'll share this with you guys. I'll walk you through how it all works. I was at a Dan Kennedy event and Armand Moran, the guy who ran the big seminar, my very first seminar went to, was speaking. He gets up on stage and he's selling the $1,000 thing and he literally closed over, I don't know, it was like four or $500,000 in sales. It was the most amazing thing I'd ever seen. And I was just watching, it was like watching someone do art. Their paintings was the most amazing thing in the world. And I'm watching this and afterwards, I'm kind of lingering around because I want to talk to him and just tell him how cool he is. And I was actually, I was speaking at his event two weeks later, first time I'd ever spoken to a big seminar, which I was excited for because that was my first event I ever went to. And so he does this pitch and he makes all the money and then at the end of it, everyone's talking and they finally leave and he walks up to me and he's got this stack of order forms that like, it looked like when you go to Staples and you buy a big old block of paper, that's what it looked like. And he held that up and he said, hey Russell, you want a stack of order forms like this? Like heck you have man? Like I would love to make a half million dollars in 90 minutes like you just did. And he said, there's one thing you gotta learn. Like all right, what's that? And he said, it's a concept I call the stack. I said, okay, what's that mean? He said, he walked back to his presentation and said, do you see how I did this thing? He walked me through what he did. And I was like, yeah, that was kind of cool. He said, in two weeks when you speak at my event, he's like, when you do, when you close, do the stack. If you do the stack, you will be rich. If you don't, you're gonna bomb. And I was like, okay. And the way he kind of showed it to me, I was like, well, it's kind of embarrassing. Like, and I'm gonna show you guys it here in a minute, but I was like, that's kind of embarrassing. He's like, just do it. I'm like, okay, like I don't want to screw this up. Like this is Armand Moore and like I was so scared, right? So I did my presentation and I just modeled his stack as close as I could remember it. Went to his event two weeks later and did over $100,000 in sales from the stage. Like by far the biggest, like I had a table rush and people springing to the back. And I was like, man, that is amazing. So I started adding the stack into my webinar presentations and it just crushed every time. Boom, my clock just crushed it, crushed it. After a while though, I remember, I thought I was too smart for the stack. I'm like, you know what, that's embarrassing. Like I don't like doing it. So I pulled it out of a presentation. First time in my life, I ever blinked on a webinar. I didn't make a single sale. And I was like, you know what? This is the lesson to me. I will never, ever, ever sell again from a webinar or stage presentation without the stack. It's my hard, fast rule. I will stack every single time. And as I've shown my students and people the stack, like it just, it works like magic every time. Like, and every time people tell me, first time I was really embarrassed and then they do it and then they make a bunch of money and suddenly they're not embarrassed anymore. It's really weird. We had a big event last week here at here and Rob for, so he hasn't made any no Rob. He was here and we were talking about webinar funnels and he raises his hand. He's like, he's kind of grumpy. He's like, Russell, I have some bad news for you. Probably good for you, but bad for me. He's like, I learned your perfect webinar and I thought it was the stupidest thing I ever heard. So two weeks ago or whatever, three weeks ago, I did a webinar to my list and I did it my way, which was the better way, the right way. And I made two sales and I was really depressed. And so I went back and I put the stack in, like you said, it was so embarrassing. I did the same webinar to the same list. We'd already seen the same offer, but hadn't bought and did it again and we made 17 sales. All he did was just change it by adding in the stack. Like it's magic, okay? And I'm giving you the idea, so I want to pre-sell and get you excited about this. We're gonna go through all the content here in a minute, but I want to understand like this wasn't something I just made up overnight. Like this is 10 years and me learning from all these different people and bringing pieces in and trying things, and testing things and that's what this has become, the perfect webinar came from that. One other kind of backstory of a cool thing that I learned, which will kind of tie more into the webinar sequences we'll talk about during the second hour. But when I was at Arman's event, he kind of pulled me aside before I spoke and he said, he's like, I'm gonna teach you one thing that'll double your sales. I'm like, all right, what's that? And he said, the biggest thing that'll impact your sales at an event outside of doing the presentation, right? He says, it's how the host of the event, how they introduce you. I'm like, really? He's like, yep. He's like, if I go to an event and the host like introduces me bad, he's like, the presentation will bomb. He said, if they come in and they introduce me the right way, then it'll make a bunch of money. And so what he started doing was actually manipulating how the introduction happened. What was said during that introduction to give him, so he would make a video ahead of time so he'd watch the video and then he'd come up on stage so he could control that pre-frame. And same thing with the webinar, I'll show you guys through the funnel, but by us positioning the funnel the way we do, I'm pre-framing that. So when someone shows up on the webinar, they're already predisposed to buy. Most people, when they show up on the webinar, and my webinar is right now closed consistently at 15% every single time, which I've heard people brag about numbers are higher, but I've never seen anyone's numbers who actually will be willing to show me their numbers. We close 15% every single time on a live webinar. And then from stage, we get pretty close to 50% every single time when I'm speaking on stage. So again, these numbers work and the process works and that's why it's so awesome. So anyway, with that said, did I pre-frame that for you guys? Are you excited to learn about this now? I hope. All right, cool. So with all that said, I always try to take these complex processes, right? All these things that we do, and like, how do I simplify them? Like I'm not like the smartest dude in the room. I don't understand that. Like I didn't do that great in college, I didn't do that great in school, but one thing I'm really, really good at is taking these super complex things and trying to build out something that I can look at and then remember things instantly, really, really quick over and over and over again. And so that's what this perfect webinar is. Every time I would tweak it and find new things and build new things and change things, I would come back and I would keep adding to this graphic until it became what it kind of is. And this template right here, in the last, man, in the last less than, I think 10 months, the last 10 months, this template here has made me personally over $10 million. I know it's made a bunch of our students like Liz Benny learned about it about a year ago. I think that she just passed $600,000 in sales from her perfect webinar. I can go person after person after person. If someone came to me and I've had people ask me, it's like Russell, what would you do if you lost your email list and you lost all your friends and you lost everything and you had to start over from scratch today and you need to become a millionaire as quick as possible, what would you do? And you only have $1,000 in your pocket or whatever, right? I always tell them the exact same thing. Like I said, first thing I would do is I would print out the perfect webinar script, then I would open up PowerPoint, I would find a product I like, I would build out the perfect webinar around that product, I would go to Facebook, I spent $1,000 in Facebook ads, driving to the perfect webinar and then I would make my first sales and whatever that gets, that first money, I take that money, reinvest more ads, reinvest more ads, reinvest more ads and within a few months I'd be a millionaire again. Like that's it. So if you guys wanna know what my secret is, it's this. Like I'm not holding anything back from you guys, like this is it, it just works and it's been tested over and over and over again. So there's the perfect webinar. Okay, so I'm gonna walk you through that. There's basically, there's three parts here in the perfect webinar. There's the introduction, which this is what we talked about like the pre-frame, like everything you're doing, like Ryan Smith gets on the webinar to capture their attention and make sure they show up. Okay, the biggest drop-off rates in webinars are two spots. One is the first 10 minutes and number two is right when you transition to your clothes. Okay, so I'm gonna show you guys things to do to strengthen those to keep people on. So first is the introduction, next is the content section and then the last is the close, is the stack, is how we close it out. Okay, so I'm gonna walk you through all three pieces of that. Okay, so the first is the introduction. Now, the biggest thing about the introduction is you cannot be boring. Okay, I used to do webinars and tell a seminar as a first guest. I listened to some of my old-tale seminars and I was just like a shy, nervous kid. So I get on and I'm like, hey, hey, everyone, can you guys hear me? If you can hear me, chat in the box over there. Okay, hey, my name is Russell. And anyway, I was just kind of like, I was so nervous. I probably lost half my audience before I even got to like anything cool, okay? You have to change that. When someone gets on a webinar, it's weird. I think about like, you're in your home office and you get on this thing and you're probably shutting the doors and you're kind of like, okay, is this gonna be worth an hour? Like, is there time for this? You've gotta grab them instantly and like just shake them and like, this is gonna be amazing, right? So the first thing I do is the second I start my webinar, I typically start about five to 10 minutes early. I get on there and I'm not just quiet. Hey guys, this is Russell. I'll be on in a little bit and quiet. I want energy and emotion and noise and things happening right when I get on. Okay, so the first thing I get on like, hey, this is Russell Brunson. You guys, I am so excited for the webinar today. If you are just logging in, we're about to get started. This webinar we're gonna be talking about, blah, blah, whatever the thing is. We're talking about a thing that's gonna be amazing and the work is starting about six and a half minutes. So six and a half minutes from right now we're gonna get started and I'm excited for you guys. We got tons of you guys logging in right now and for those of you guys that are just jumping on, I wanna welcome you to the webinar. My name's Russell Brunson. I'm gonna be your host today and we're about five minutes away from go time and I'm just excited to have you guys here. And when the webinar starts, I'm gonna be covering one of my favorite topics in the world which is blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, okay? Now for those of you guys who are just jumping on, I'm gonna welcome you to the webinar. We're about four and a half minutes from, you see how I'm doing this? So there's constant talking for 10 minutes where I'm just talking in a loop because people are logging in every about 30 seconds. Well, you'll see, like if you have a big webinar, like every second new people are logging in, okay? And so I'm on like a little 20 to 30 second track where I'm just looping this and looping this and looping. I'm trying to say it different ways so it's a little interesting and exciting, but I'm telling them how much longer it is, what's the hook, why is this gonna be exciting, why am I so excited, why should they be excited? And I just keep going through that, I'm looping that and looping that. And you'll notice when you jump on a webinar, like that where I'm introing it or someone that understands this process is doing it, instantly there's a different change in energy. Like you get on and you're like, wow, like this is, this is gonna be cool, like something's happening here, right? As opposed to like, hey everyone, we're gonna start in a few minutes. Hey, we're testing the audio, we'll try to get on in a few more minutes. You know what I mean? It just changes the whole state, right? I'm very big in like, if you guys have ever studied NLP, talks a lot about pre-frames and states and things like that, like I can affect your state really, really easily based on a frame that I'm taking you through, right? So if I want you to buy, if I'm gonna be asking you for money over here, everything else is happening before here is a frame, right? Each, every time you move from one step and you're entering through a frame, and so I wanna control that frame the best I can and make that experience amazing, because the better that is, the more likely you're gonna buy, you're gonna do it on the next page, on the next page and keep moving through, right? So it starts with me with a whole bunch of energy, getting excited for what's about to happen, okay? All right, now when the webinar's about to start, I'm like, hey guys, we are at the top of the hour, we always start in time, I'm ready to get started, I hope you guys ready to get started, let's jump right into this. Okay, my name, again, and even though I've said it 50 times, I'm starting again, my name is Russell Brunson, we're welcome you guys to the webinar today. Now, we're gonna be on for between 16 and 90 minutes and before this webinar ends, blah. Okay, I'm giving them a bold promise. I have to give them a ruler by which to measure success by. Okay, if you don't give people a ruler to measure their success by, then what happens? At the end of the webinar, they make up their own way to judge success. And nine times out of 10, it's not gonna be pretty, okay? So I have to tell them at the very beginning, like this is what success will be like at the end of this webinar, okay? So I might say something like, let me grab my slides so you can see. I've done this presentation more times than almost anything on earth, so I'll just kinda show you some of these things. Okay, can you see my screen there? All right, so first thing I'm doing, okay, so during this presentation, I'm gonna show you guys my weird niche funnel, it's currently making me $17,000 per day, which is pretty awesome, right? But the cool thing, because that's cool for me, doesn't really matter for you, I'm gonna show you guys how to ethically knock it off in less than 10 minutes. How many of you guys wanna learn how to ethically knock off my funnel in less than 10 minutes? Okay, I just made him a bold promise. I'm gonna show you a funnel, it's making $18,000 a day, and I'm gonna show you guys how to knock it off. That's it. Now they know, like, at the end of this webinar, he's gonna show me his funnel, he's gonna go ahead and knock it off. If I get those two things, this hour has been successful. Okay, does that make sense, you guys? So you've gotta give him a bold promise, because that's what they're gonna judge you by. So many times people jump in the webinar, start teaching at the end of it, people are like, why was I even on? That thing was so weird, right? So you gotta do that. All right, so bold promise. Second thing is we gotta hook to the end. So if you look at mine, down here, slide number six, I say, now I gotta give for you guys. For everyone who stays till the end, I created a sales funnel called Funalology, and I'm gonna give you this funnel for free. In fact, this one looks like right here, see those four pages, it sells a product here. I'm gonna give you guys this as a gift for free, just for staying till the end. Nothing else, you have to pay anything, you get this for free if you stay till the end. Okay, what do I do? Well, I've got their attention, cause I only got it for a few minutes before a lot of them are gonna start jumping off. I've gotta grab them, and I've got to give them a hook to the end. Why should you stay till the end? Okay, there's not to be a free funnel. Sometimes like one of my webinars, one of my best selling ones was my high ticket secrets. And I say, how many of you guys wanna see my call center? Okay, it's a little tiny two person operation. Am I cool? What I'm gonna do is that everyone who stays thin, I'm gonna pick up my laptop, we're gonna walk over there to the call center, you guys can check it out and see exactly what it looks like. Okay, and people are going nuts, they wanted to see it, right? So it doesn't have to be something tangible, it can help. You know, I've seen a lot of people go like, at the end of this, I'm gonna give away a free iPod, which I'm not a big fan of those, cause it's so unrelated, and now you get people staying just for whatever. But if you can find something that's just kind of interesting and unique that's something that, you know what, I'm willing to sell to you, cause they're basically selling you their time. I'm gonna sell you this time in the hopes that I'll get the sing at the end. Okay, so hooking at the end is big key. Commanding attention, this starts from the second you start the webinar, me talking, loud, energy, you know, commanding attention. Then you come in to qualifying yourself, like why should they care about you? So I come here and I'm like, hey, I'm Russell, here's me Tony Robbins, me, Richard Branson, here's me sleeping, and I just kinda like, I'm showing them that they should care that I'm the one presenting. Okay, so I'm qualifying myself, and then I future pace. Okay, future pacing is all about kind of putting themselves into what it could be. So for me, I future pace a little bit earlier right here. So I tell my story, this is kinda like my Ernie's disclaimer slash future pacing. So I'm saying, look, by the end of this webinar, I promise you guys you will not make $17,000 a day. In fact, when I got started, I just wanted to cover some bills. One day I was listening to tell seminar arts, someone told me how they were a millionaire, and I thought, I wanna be a millionaire. And so this time I become a millionaire. You make $2,000 a day, and for me, I started making these little funnels, and most of them flop, but every once in a while one would work, and some made $10 a day, some made $100 a day, and then every once in a while one blows up. And I got one right now that makes $27,000, Ernie, $17,000 per day. Right now, my company got 27 funnels. Can you imagine me just have one funnel working for you? And then imagine we have two, then imagine we have 10, and then imagine we have 27. Okay, we're doing over $10 million a year because we have 27 funnels. And you're not gonna start that way, but you're gonna start with one. Can you imagine what it'll be like when you have a bunch of them? Okay, so I'm future pacing. Imagine when this outcome, that's true for me right now, is true for you. What will that be like? Okay, and that's my introduction. So does that make sense, you guys? Do you see how that introduction, now you're like, man, this is gonna be amazing. I wanna see how it works. All right, good questions. Okay, all right, so that's kind of my introduction. And you guys have fun with it, you don't have to follow, like don't be like me, just follow it to a T. I always try making fun, interesting, and then I always see everyone just copies me. So for me, I talk about this, and I'm like, hey, I'm gonna make a promise. Promise me that after I show you this, you're not gonna use these powers for evil. And everyone laughs. And then two weeks later, I see this on everybody's intro slide, like hey, promise me you're not gonna use these powers for evil. I'm like, don't copy me. Think, plug something else into here that's cool, that's more fitting for your personality, you know? Anyway, so have fun with the introduction, just have a lot of energy. If you do that, that's gonna keep you on, because that's gonna be your big drop off points. I remember back with Alex Mendozian's tell-seminar course. He showed a tell-seminar timeline. It was like 10 minutes to drop off point, and 15 minutes to top off points. It's like, you gotta get them past that 10 minute mark, and you gotta keep them past the 15, 15 minute mark. If you can do those two things, you just increase how much money you make, because that's the two drop off points. So introduction is big. All right, now we're gonna transition over here to the content. And this is something that's, this is like, for me, was the missing piece. I used to do an intro, and then I would just talk for like an hour, and then I would go over into the stack and the closes. And I learned this concept from a guy named Jason Flatlin. Some of you guys may know him. He's got a webinar product, and the webinar product was good. It was really good. But the biggest thing I got out of it was how to structure the content. And I've changed how I do it a little bit, but definitely, I was heavily influenced by what he was doing, okay? Because you can just teach to teach, but if you teach the right way, if you teach this way, this will, every single thing you're doing will set up to sell at the end, and it'll make, it'll have people leave feeling amazing. Okay? All right, so here's kind of the concept. First thing, and I'm a huge believer in this, is your webinar has to focus on one thing. What is the one thing that you're trying to help somebody to accomplish? Okay? I had a conversation with Perry Belcher, one of my friends, and he was talking about, he was a great copywriter, but he was talking about how he had gone, and they'd done a bunch of tests on their different sales videos, and webinars, and things they had done. He said that, he said that they found is that if they ever tried to convince somebody of more than one thing in a sales video, so of two things, or three things, or whatever that was, that it would, nine hands out of ten would bomb. And the more simple the concept was, and there was only one thing, like the offers they made, and the most money, the one thing that all in common was, they were all focused on one thing. One thing you're trying to convince somebody of. Okay? And so for you, your webinar's not about, you know, I'm gonna show you that the seven different ways to increase traffic to your website, like that's not it. It's understanding like, well the one thing, what's the one thing that they've gotta understand? If they believe that one thing, then everything else fits in, everything else will work. Okay, so for me, my one thing I'm trying to convince them of, is that they can quickly create sales funnels. That's my one thing. Okay? So it's kind of, I kind of, you know, it never says that like as a thing, so it's kind of wrapped in this headline. So the headline's my weird niche funnel that makes me 17,947 dollars per day, and how to effort to knock off in 10 minutes. Okay, the point of that headline, is what's the one thing you're gonna learn? You're gonna learn, first off, what a good funnel is, and how to knock it off. Okay, how to create your own funnel. Like that's my one thing. Okay, so for you, you gotta feel like, what's the one belief you're trying to get someone? And then, inside of that, we're embedding three secrets. Secret one, secret two, and secret three. Okay? So each of these secrets, the goal of those secrets, is not just to again teach random stuff, is to identify what their false belief patterns are, and then shatter them. And after it's been shattered, then rebuild it. Okay? So let me show you, let me pull up. We had a whiteboard here right now, I would just whiteboard this, but we don't, so we open up my paint program. I can doodle some stuff for you here. All right, this is key if you can understand this part of it, right? All right, so here's my one thing. My one thing. And from here, we got three secrets. So the first thing I figure out is, for somebody to believe my one thing, right? Because my whole goal is, if I get them, remember I told you before, the only goal of a presentation is to get them to believe that what you have will work for them. That's it. Okay, if I get you to believe that what I have will work for you, you will give me money. Okay? So the one thing, this is what they got to believe. Okay? Now look at that, now that I know this is what they gotta believe. They gotta believe that eating butter for blood, breakfast is the secret to losing weight, right? That's the one thing. Then I gotta figure out, what are the three faults, belief patterns they have based on that? Okay? If the one thing is like that they need to use Facebook to try, Facebook's the best source to generate leads online. So I know that's what it is. Then what are my three faults, what are the three faults, belief patterns that people have about that? Okay? One thing I wanna add to this too, if you're struggling for what your one thing is, one of the easiest ways to do it is if you can fill in the sentence right here, it'll probably work. How to blank without blank. Okay? So right here, how to blank, this is what they want. So how to drive unlimited leads to your website, how to lose a million pounds in five minutes, how to, whatever it is they want, so here's their want without the thing that they hate. Without cutting out carbs, right? How to make, like one of my best selling webinars is High Ticket Secrets, is how to sell super high expensive things on the phone without ever, or how to sell super high ticket packages without ever talking on the phone. Okay? They all wanna sell just high things without the thing that they hate is talking on the phone. Right? So think about that, how to do whatever the coolest thing is you have without the thing that they hate. Okay, that's my headline, nine times out of 10 on a webinar, okay? And ties back to this one thing, okay? So now I gotta figure out the three, the three false belief patterns, okay? So let me come back here, so I'll show you this here in my webinar. So transition here, I go through, here's my three secrets, okay? So secret number one is how to ethically sell over a million dollars with the funnel hacks from your competitors for under a hundred dollars. Okay, number two is how to clone the proven funnel in less than 10 minutes. Number three is how to get their same customers to start coming to your website instead, okay? So for me, my three false belief patterns, let me kind of show this in, so you can see how this kind of relates back to my presentation, right? So first belief pattern that people have is that for you to be able to create a good sales funnel, like that it's really hard. Okay, like you've gotta go through and have, you know, you've gotta have designers and programmers and you have to like test a million things and it's gonna be really hard, right? So that creating a sales funnel is hard, okay? So that's the belief pattern right here, is that this is really, really hard. Okay, so my goal, my job with this is to basically take that belief that they have that's hard and have to smash it. It is not hard. In fact, it's easy. So I, with this right here, it says, I gotta break it and then rebuild it. Okay, so it's really, really hard. So I break that during the content and then I rebuild it. Okay, next thing here. So the next one is that it takes a lot of time. Oh, it takes so much time, so hard to build a sales funnel. So that's their belief. I have to smash that belief and then I rebuild the new belief, okay? And the third one is it's really hard, even if I had a funnel, it would be hard to get people to come to my website. So for me, traffic, traffic is really, really hard. So that's their belief. I have to smash that belief and show them how it's really, really easy. Okay, now if I've done that effectively, I've been this, this and this, it then thereby proves that this one thing is true. Does that make sense, you guys? I know we're going into the deep concepts and strategies here, which makes me laugh because I was the worst student of all time. I hated school, I really struggled with it. And teachers would talk about somebody that was so boring to me, but this is fascinating because this equals insane amounts of money. Okay, grasping this and understanding this will make you a millionaire faster than anything else I know on earth. Okay, so that's why I'm spending time with it, why I want you to understand it. The first one, how to ethically steal over a million dollars with a funnel to actually be competitors for under $100. Okay, so now I've got to help them understand this. Like, I just modeled people who are successful. Because if they don't have that belief yet, I wanna give them that false belief so I can destroy it. Boom, which strengthens my one thing. Then it transitions to the last one, I'm like, okay, so how many of you guys think this is amazing, but you're like, well, this is cool, Russell, but how do I get people to come to my website? It's really, really hard, right? Because that's what they believe, it's the last belief pattern, if I can shatter this one, they've got no excuses, they have to give me money, because this is the only last excuse they could possibly have. So the third excuse here, I can't get traffic, because I show them, here is how easy is it to get traffic, I show them a really simple process. Now again, notice that I showed one, okay? My mistake that I would have done in the past, and that most of you guys are gonna do, like let me show you 12 ways to get traffic, and you're showing all these things, that's wrong, you gotta show them one. Again, it comes back to the one thing, the one thing is the core to the webinar, but it's also the core to each secret, like what's the one thing of the secret, the one thing, the one thing is like, should be the overarching theme of entire presentations, everything's like the one thing, right? So within the secret, what's the one thing they gotta do to get traffic? Cause I can show you 50 ways to get traffic, but I'm not doing that, I'm showing you as the one, that's gonna be the simplest and the easiest. So I show them this, they go to similar web, they find out where their competitor's traffic's coming from, and they just go and reverse engineer it, and then look how easy it is, and in five minutes, my ads are on the same site, and if I model it worked, do you guys think that you can be successful with this as well? They're like, yes I can. So I took their last belief, we smashed it, now they've got no legs to stand on. The only thing we can believe now is that this one thing, oops, this one thing is true, and if they believe this one thing is true, then they have to give me money, unless they don't have it, but if they have money, I've just destroyed the three belief patterns that we're keeping them from giving me money, and we're keeping them from believing that this would work for them, and I obliterated them. Now that they're gone, right? It's it, they have to buy now. Okay, I've taken away all the friction, all those kind of things, and that's how we teach the content, you guys, okay? My students started doing that, my webinar closing rates went from, you know, five to six percent up to 10, 15, and then like I said, live stage we're closing about 50% now from a room, which is amazing, walking in a room with 1000 people, and you're like, half these people will buy before I'm done, half million bucks, it's really exciting, right? And so that's what's really important to understand you guys is that. When I smash these and obliterate them, they have to believe my one thing, which means they have to give me money, okay? All right, so there we go. Now we've gone through the content stuff. We have broke their belief patterns and rebuilt the ones that we need them to have. Now we got to transition to where we ask for money, okay? Now this typically is like the most awkward and embarrassing part for most people. In fact, it was fun, I went to like a network marketing meeting, one of my friends is like, hey, we're having a little home party, would you like to come? And for me, I was just like, yes, I do. What time? So we went to this thing, right? And then we show up, there's like five or six people and they were selling essential oils, right? So everyone's trying these oils and sniffing them, they do a presentation talking about it. And the lady up front talking, she's doing a great presentation. Like it was really, really good. And all of a sudden you could tell that like, now she needed to ask people for money. And like her face gets red and she starts talking really, really fast and she's sweating and she gets nervous. And the more nervous she gets, the more nervous she gets. And it keeps just like compounding and building on herself and she's freaking out and she's like, ah, you know what am I gonna do? And then she's like, oh, you know, if you want to buy, you can go over there and buy, but you know, I probably wouldn't buy it because most people shouldn't buy it. And it's just, you know, but if you are interested in giving us money, you should definitely talk to us. But if not, don't worry about it. I was like, whoa, what did she just blur it out? Like, I was so painful. And I'm probably, for most of you guys, that's how it's probably gonna be. And it was for me, I would get to the selling part and I'd get nervous and I'd like, and I already speak fast anyway. Like all of a sudden, all of a sudden my voice would just like, like would speed up even faster. And so I had to like, I had to figure out like, how do we transition this? And Armand taught me this. He said, this is the easiest transition in the world. After you've gone through everything, then all you say is, hey, let me ask you guys a question. That's the transition, like that's it. Okay, so let me ask you guys a question. We've been talking for the last 60 minutes and I think it's been a good experience. We've had a lot of fun. I taught you guys a lot of cool things, but you know, 60 minutes, it's harder to cover everything in 60 minutes. So I have a question for you. How many of you guys, how many of you guys would like to have, you know, or how many of you guys would be interested in, if I had a way for you to be able to continue this conversation and be able to continue, continue what you learned today, how many of you guys would be interested? Everyone's like, oh, they're going crazy. And then for me, I like asking permission. So I would say, well, if you don't mind, I put together a special package today. So here's my, let me ask you a question picture. In fact, this is how I do mine. And don't, I have to say 20 people copy this picture. Like go find your own picture, you guys. This is mine. Anyway, but conceptually, this is what I say, say it. So let me ask you guys a question. How many of you guys feel a little bit like this right now? Or I show this picture and then I laugh and I'm like, I'm like, I know I talk really, really fast. You guys, I'm guessing that, you know, last 60 minutes has been fun, but some of you guys probably feel a little bit like this. Am I right? I'm like, yes, am I okay? I totally get that. So what I wanted to do because of that is we put together a special package called Funnel Hacks. And Funnel Hacks is a way for you guys to be able to implement this stuff over the next six weeks. And I just want to ask you guys permission, are you okay if I take a minute and kind of share with you what's inside of Funnel Hacks? And then I wait. And it's always awkward. Okay, but you gotta be okay with awkward. I learned this from Joel Bauer, again, one of the public speaking classes I went through. He said, you always ask permission before you sell. Because as soon as they said, yes, you can sell to me, I guess it's changed everything, right? So I always ask some permission. So do you guys mind if I just spend a minute real quick and show you this package, particularly all Funnel Hacks? And then I wait. I do this awkward silence. And if I'm in a seminar, also you hear people say, yes, yes, they're screaming, I was like, yes, yes, you just tell us what it is. Like there's all start screaming. And then I know I'm in. Kind of webinar, I sit there. And all of a sudden, all of a sudden I start seeing all these people and then go to webinar saying like, okay, okay, I'm in, I'm in, I'm in. And they give you permission. If someone just gave you permission to sell, they're more likely to buy. So I like to ask them for permission. All right, so there's probably permission. Then I go into, here's what we got. Now we start going over here into the stack and then the closes. Now I'm gonna go through the stack because this is the most important. I'm gonna share some of these closes. I'm not gonna go through all the closes because this is what, there's 16 of them here. I can spend like 20 minutes on each one and we don't have time for that. Also like there's a lot more in this. This is just the ones I use like all the time. And then there's a whole bunch more that I use sometimes. And there's, so I'm not gonna go into all of these, but if I'll show you guys a bunch of them. If you're interested in learning all of these, I have a presentation on the perfect webinar I can wait for free that goes into all of these. And you can go, if you just go to perfectwebinarsecrets.com you can get that as DVD and it goes through all of the different closes. So, just because I know I'm not gonna be able to have time to go through all of them. I'll show you a bunch of them, but just if you wanted to dig deep into that, you can get that for free. It's like 495 shipping handling emails at DVD out to you that goes through all of them. Okay, so the stack. So this is the stack that I learned from Arm and Morn. Now, for this stack to make sense, the key concept behind this Arm and Morn taught me, he said that most speakers get on stage, right? And then I, okay, this is what my package is. Boom, number, first thing you get is this. And then you get this, and you get this, and you get this, and they show you all these cool things. And then the last thing they show you is usually like just one of their bonuses, right? You're gonna get this really cool calculator watch or whatever, right? And then you're like, okay, and all it costs is $10,000. And so the problem is that people's attention span is like super short, right? Like, shorter than you will ever believe, right? People's attention span is just like almost not there. And so when you say the price is only $10,000, the only thing that they associate that price with is the last bonus you showed them. So if you showed them all this cool stuff, like you're gonna get a car and a house and an airplane and a, you know, I'm gonna name Mars after you, and you're gonna get this cool calculator watch. And the price is only $10,000. All they're like is like, all they remember is like, oh, it's 10 grand for a calculator watch. And that sucks, that's a lot of money, right? You say that's how people's brains work. And so what you have to do is you have to restack what's in the offer over and over and over again. And right before you reveal the price, you have to restack again so they associate the price with everything, okay? So I'll show you how that works in a minute, but if you see from these little images here, like here I'm saying, component number one is this, then I talk about it, and I say, okay, now component number two, you get this plus this, I talk about that. And so that means you're gonna get this plus this plus this, and I talk about it, and I see I keep stacking, I keep restacking it, then I show them the price, and I move on, I'll talk about this stuff here in a minute, okay? So let me show you how that works. So I come through this presentation, and I talk about the first thing. So this is my stack slide, so I say, okay. Okay, so the way I do that, is through a process called if all statements. I learned this from Dave Van Hoos. Okay, if all statements. So what I do that to say, this package is $11,000. Now obviously I'm not gonna charge you as $11,552, I think it's worth it, I'm not gonna charge you as that. But I need to ask you guys a question. If all that the funnel hacks package did was, so if all, if all that this did was, blah. Would it be worth it? If all this did was blank, would it be worth it? If all this did was blank, would it be worth it? So three times, I'm trying to get three yeses, yes, yes, yes. I need them to say yes three times, that if all that this package did was something, and it'd be worth $11,000, I can say yes three times. So first off, if all this did was finally give you fun, it was profitable, would it be worth it? Yes. If all this did was to help you to share your message with more people, would it be worth it? Yes. If all this did was give you the ability to cancel all the monthly tools you're currently using to glue together your funnels, would it be worth it? Yes. Yes, yes, yes. Three times, they told me it's worth $11,552. Okay? So now when I come back to them and say it's only a thousand, if they don't buy it, then they're lying to themselves. They just told me it's worth 11 grand. I'm giving them a 90% discount. Okay, you see why that's important? So I'm taking this price, the top value, and then I have to get them to, in their own mind, to say it's worth that. I do that through if all statements. Now take this last slide, this slide like I said, I tested so many things here, in fact, I'm gonna make it live so you can see exactly what I do. Okay, so this is some cool stuff. All right, first off, you see that all pretty well. First off, I recap my offer on the slide. The reason why I do that is because whenever I get the slide, everybody asks me, many times, what was in it again, what's in it again? I'm like, are you kidding me? I've stacked this 80 times, how in the world do you not know what's in my package at this point in our relationship, right? But they don't, so I have it here so they can just see it again, okay? I have the big old price in green because green means go. I have a call to action which is whatever.com slash go, because I want them to go. I have a countdown clock. Now, we started with these countdown clocks when I would speak at live events. I put a 10 or 15 minute countdown clock, and it would get people's butts out of their seat and run you to the back. So I started doing it with webinars, and what we did initially is we would do, I think I did a 10 minute countdown clock, and I would just be talking during the 10 minutes, and while I was talking during this 10 minutes, sales would be coming in, and the second the clock hit zero, sales stopped, just every time. I was like, God, that's annoying. So then we made a clock that went 15 minutes, and sales came in at 15 minutes, they just, the second the clock hit zero, people stopped buying. I was like, well, screw that, let's make this a 30 minute clock, so we bumped it out to 30 minutes, and then I sit on for 30 minutes doing Q and A, and the whole 30 minutes people are buying, and usually about 30 minutes is when it starts trickling down, it stops. But it's crazy to me, I did webinars for almost a decade where I would do the close, say the price, and I'd end, where I know that more than half my, I say it's probably three fourth of my sales, come during this 28 minute, during this 30 minute countdown clock, okay? Like it has made me so much more money, I don't know how I didn't do it before, but it's amazing, right? People are like, how do you make a countdown clock in PowerPoint? I just found a clock, they're on a clock online, I recorded in Camtasia, turned into a video file, and I added the video to my slide, it's just a video file, so you can pause it, and push play, like it's not a real countdown clock, it's a little video, because I can figure out how to make a countdown clock, so there you go, that's what I did. So countdown clock's huge. Then the next question is like, what do you do for 30 minutes while the countdown clock's happening, okay? Same thing you do in the introduction, you've got to talk constantly, I got to keep the energy high, keep them pumped up, keep them excited, because if I have a lull, they're gonna stop buying, okay? So I can't have a lull, so what I do is, I went through after this webinar 30 or 40 times, I know what everybody's top questions are, so I just preload them here in my slides, and they go through, and I answer questions for people that have in GoToWebinar. Okay, all right, so you guys getting this? Is it making sense to you? All right, couple other things. One thing I just did to you guys right there, I just dropped three trial closes on you. So some of you guys are saying, what is a trial close? A trial close is something that I am embedding all throughout my presentations. I learned this from Ted Thomas. Ted Thomas' nickname is the Pied Piper. I've seen videos of him speaking at Robert Kiyosaki events, where he will have a line of a thousand people all holding the credit cards above their head, waving it, following him to the back of the room. And they call him Pied Piper, because he's the best at like getting people to do that. And so I was speaking at an event, and I had heard rumors of Ted Thomas, never met him. I'm doing my presentation, I look out over the eyes, and in the back there, I see this old man, and I was like, I think that's Ted Thomas. I am so nervous right now that the king of closing is sitting here watching me speak, right? So I did my presentation, and we did some cells, and it wasn't like amazing, but it was good. And I was like kind of proud of it, and he was kind of hanging out after the webinar, excuse me, the seminar, and then after everyone kind of disappeared, he came to me and said, hey Russell, my name's Ted Thomas. I'm like, oh yeah, I know who you are, don't worry. And he's like, can I take you to lunch? I'm like, that would be awesome. So he took me to lunch, and we start talking, he starts asking me all these questions, question after question after question, and then he stops, and he says, Russell, look at your head right now. And I looked, and my head's like just nodding. He's like, see how your head's moving? I'm like, yeah. He's like, what I just did is I just did a whole bunch of trial closes, which is why you are nodding yes right now. He's like, when I was in your room, said you were a gauging speaker, you were getting people to buy, people went and bought, things were good. But I was sitting in the back of the room, and I noticed that not a single person's head was moving in the entire room. He said, if you watch me speak, from the second I stand on stage, the entire presentation, everybody's head's nodding yes the whole time. I'm like, really? He's like, yeah, they've said yes to me about a million times before I ever asked him for money. I was like, well, how do you do that? This little thing I call trial closes. He said, what trial closes is you ask people little questions, little yes or no questions, and the answer is obviously yes. And they say yes, and they say yes again. And even if you're home in a webinar, your head is probably nodding when I'm doing my webinar. So I'm using these trial closes throughout the entire thing. So for example, here's my intro slide. So hey guys, welcome to the webinar. I'm talking about my weird niche phone. It's currently making me $17,947 today. How many guys would love to see behind the scenes of a funnel that's making 17 grand a day? Wouldn't that be awesome? Okay, well, I'm gonna show you guys that. You guys excited for that? Okay, I said three trial closes. They told me yes three times reading the first part of the headline. Okay, and I say, but not only am I gonna teach you that, I'm gonna show you guys something that's even better for you, because other things cool for me, right? Another trial close. But I'm gonna show you guys something. I'm gonna show you guys how you can ethically knock it off in 10 minutes. How many of you guys wanna learn how to ethically knock off my funnels in less than 10 minutes? Boom, got another yes. Okay, so that's six yeses in my very first slide. Now I need you guys to do me a favor before I show you this. Can you guys please promise you you're not gonna use these powers for evil? What do they say? Yes, okay? Over here, okay. So what I'm gonna do is, I gotta show you an Error Disclamer. First off, I promise you guys you are not gonna make $17,000 per day, right? Now you guys probably knew that you weren't gonna do that, right? Boom, two trial closes. Now, and I start going through it. And so every time I'm saying something, I'm interacting with the audience and I'm saying, does this make sense? You guys getting this? Awesome? You guys excited? Does that make sense? Right? Right? And they're saying yes, yes. Like I should cancel it. If you can't, all my webinars, I've probably asked you to say yes at least a thousand times in my presentation, not more. Every time people do, want me to critique a webinar, I start watching within five minutes. I know exactly what's wrong. They never, they didn't interact with you. They didn't say yes. And because usually they're embarrassed, they feel stupid or whatever, but that is what gets people to nod their head. Okay, and I need them nodding their head the entire 90 minutes if I'm gonna ask them for money. Couple of other cool things that trial closes work the best for is when you show testimonials. Okay, so for example, this is Liz, right? So a lot of times people show testimonial and they move on. Like here's Liz's testimony. When you share a testimonial, you've got a future pace and drop a million trial closes after every single one. So I read Liz's thing. This is what Liz said. Isn't that amazing? Like what would you do if you had 100 new clients this month? Now I'm sure some of you guys are thinking that's probably, you know, that's not likely for me, right? And I get that. Like a lot of you guys that may not make a lot of sense might not be possible, but I promise you if you had asked Liz a few months ago if that would be possible for her, she would have told you no too. For you guys to be able to say, well, how good does my webinar have to be, Russell? Kind of my experience is if you can get a webinar that closes at a, you know, seven or eight percent, like that's a good webinar, you're gonna make a lot of money with that. You get 10%, it's a million dollar webinar, you get above that, you know, I mean, we're at 15%, and it's been live less than a year, and we're just shy of $10 million for this one webinar, and it's not slowing down at all, like it's amazing, so that kind of should give you some groundwork, okay? Why is it so important to test these things and to try them over and over and over and over again? Why is it important to learn the skills? Because shifting, you know, two or three percentage points is the difference between a seven figure your company and eight figure your company. Like it's just, it's those little tiny tweaks all added in together, okay? All right, so next thing we're gonna cover, so that's the webinar when somebody gets on, right? So the next piece we wanna cover is, what do we, how do we get someone on the webinar? What's the process for that? So let me shrink this screen, and... And Michael asked, how does Russell identify the top three beliefs to kill? Does he just go for the obvious ones? So I was wondering if you could kind of shed some light on your three secrets, like which three beliefs do you usually shoot for? Great question. Okay, so the question is, what three beliefs do I usually shoot for? So for me, well, it depends on what, like what is I'm selling, so it's gotta tie into that, right, somehow. It's gotta weave back, you know, for me, like, the reason why I am showing, let me find my slide real quick. So many slides. Where's my number one? Okay, like the reason why I'm showing you guys my process of this is because I'm trying to get them, you know, like, this helps my case for, like if they learn how to funnel act someone, then they're gonna wanna use click funnels. If they then learn how to use click funnels, they wanna use click funnels. If they believe they can get traffic, they're funneling them on how to use click funnels, right? So it's like, it's gotta support what it is I'm selling at the end of the day. So that's the first part. The second part of it, though, is most people have the same issues, and they're usually tied to time, to money, to things like that. And so I try to figure out how to base it. So I don't have like a hard, fast rule for that really. A lot of it's just kind of knowing your market, knowing like what it is that keeps them from taking action, you know? But I would venture to guess that it's pretty similar in most markets, right? Like, let's say it's the weight loss market, for example, and everyone's tried a million different diets. So why, and they're not a success in the past. One of them is like, they, you know, maybe they heard they had to get rid of carbs, and so they love donuts, they're not giving up donuts or bread or whatever, right? So it's like, if you know that, that's the majority of people's concern, you say, look, the cool thing is, you can do this without cutting out carbs. Like, are you serious? Like, I cannot cut out carbs. Like, how is this possible, right? Or, you know, they've tried in the past, and they lost it, but they gained it right back. Okay, that's a common concern. So, okay, how do we make this what's the lifestyle change, not a yo-yo diet, or whatever those things are? So I think it really, you know, the key is understanding your market, and understanding like what pain they've gone through to get to where they're at right now, right? People take action, they get on a webinar, they're buying things because they're usually in some kind of pain, and they're trying to get out of that pain. And so if you can identify, like, the last 10 times they tried to get out of that pain, like, what was the issues they probably ran into? Like, I know with my market, you know, the issues were, they don't know what to do, okay, which is why we showed them how to funnel hack, now you know what to do. I don't know how to do it, I can't afford programmers, so, okay, well that's cool, let me show you how to do it with ClickFunnels, and like, okay, well that's cool, but even if I did do it right, I can't get traffic, okay, let me show you that too. You know, like, those are the three core things that my people struggle with, and so for your market, you've got to think about it, like, what are the, you know, the last 10 things they've tried in the past, like, why do they fail, why do they think they failed? If you can answer that, then it becomes really easy. So, yeah, I hope that kinda helps. Yeah, and I'll just kind of chime in here real quick. It's really, really simple to come up with your three core concepts, and 95% of the time, it's gonna come down to either time or money. How can you save them time? How can you save them money or make them more money? And then you usually throw one other thing in there. So, I mean, even with Secret Number One, you know, how to steal over a million dollars of funnel hacks for under a hundred bucks? Like, how to make more money without spending a whole lot of money? You know, how to clone the proven funnel in less than 10 minutes? Like, how to get this stuff done in very little time, and then you usually just throw in one other one that, like, common pain point that you know that they're having. So, yeah man, keep it up, man, you're doing an awesome job, so we got a lot of people that are excited, so I just wanna bring that question to your attention. Very cool, awesome, okay. All right, so let's transition. I got another 40 minutes.
