So, I'm gonna pull this down here now. So, this is one of like 10 different registration pages. I'm gonna show you guys a whole bunch of them here, just so you can get different ideas what we're doing and how we're doing it. But, this is one of my, this is the control, this one is, made us a lot of money. And so, a couple things about it. The first thing I want you to notice, is you notice right here, we got the one thing in the three secrets. So, if you look at my page, like, the only copy on this page almost is, here's my one thing, and down here are my three secrets. Right? If you thought through your one thing and your three secrets a lot, like, that's gonna be the best copy you've got. So, that's basically my landing page. That's number one. Number two, the thing that gets people to register for a webinar or join a squeeze page or whatever that is, is curiosity. And, excuse me, curiosity and pain, those are the two things, right? And so, I need them to, like, whatever my one thing is, whatever my headline is for this thing, it has to be something they cannot figure out in their mind on their own, okay? And so, for my, it says, weird niche funnel is currently making me $17,000 per day and how to ethically knock it off in less than 10 minutes. Okay, like, the human mind looks at that and they're like, does it make any sense? How could someone make 17 grand a day? And how could I ethically, does it make, like, how could I ethically knock it off in less than 10 minutes? Like, there's so much curiosity wrapped in that thing that you have to, like, if you read that for the rest of your life, you'd be thinking, like, what the crap, like, what was that thing again? I wish I could find that webinar because I gotta figure out what his weird niche funnel is. Like, I gotta see what that thing is, right? It was funny, we did our certification program last week and one of the camera guys, he was a, he's a, my main camera guy brought his, his like, intern to come and help him, he's a 16-year-old kid and the kid, like, afterwards came to me and was like, hey, I have a question for you. So I saw one of you, I saw this webinar thing online on Facebook, whatever, he's like, it said that you make $17,000 per day, is that true? And I was like, I was like, actually, like, we make a lot more than that, but one of our phones makes 17 grand a day, yes. He was like, oh, that's so cool. And he was like, so excited because he didn't think it was true and then I found out it was true, then he was just like, like going nuts because he wanted to figure it out, right? And so they see this, like, it's just, it's curious, like, I can't figure that out. Okay, some people all the time that are like, that their headlines are like, the fastest way to lose weight. Okay, the problem is that I can think like, what's the fastest way to lose weight? Probably cutting out carbs. Okay, in my head, I think I can answer it. So then I don't register because I think I can answer it. You have to believe it. So curious, they can't just answer it in their head. If you do that, it's like, it's gonna register and gets them to show up. Okay, sometimes people register, they don't show up because it's not curious enough. Okay, so that's the key guys is you gotta, you gotta like, invoke curiosity. Like that's, I can't stress that enough. If all of our split tests that we've done over the last five years, like what I found consistently across the board is that the more curiosity based the headline is on the landing page, the more people will respond if it's opting in, registering for register or whatever. Okay, that's it. Okay, and so again, that ties in the hole now. That becomes the title of my presentation. That becomes my one thing that ties into my three secrets. So all those pieces kind of come on here. Couple other things. Notice this picture back here. So you see this picture, like what does it look like? It doesn't make any sense, right? Okay, there's a theme here. Like here's me doing the hang loose sign. Is that a phone? Is the hang loose? So I'm doing this like pictures like a website. The background is this huge website back there. Like what is that? Right? It's just raw curiosity. I don't know what this is. We tested tons of different pictures back here. Like beach scenes, all sorts of things. This one crushes them every single time. I think it's because like it doesn't make any sense. Right? Like what in the world is that? It doesn't make any sense. Okay, so there's kind of your theme. The less sense you make on your registration page and the more curiosity based you are, the more people are gonna opt in. So there's my hard, fast rule for that. All right, so that is one of our registration pages. I'm gonna come here and click funnels and... Boom, I think this is the right one. Okay, so for me in my market, like I know that for my webinar, like this is like the majority of my market I'm going after. These are internet marketers, it's business opportunity seekers, it's people who, this would be interesting to them, right? But I know that my product, as I scale this and grow it, I know there's gonna be other markets that I also wanna go after that this message isn't gonna resonate with them, right? Like a chiropractor is gonna see this and they're not gonna resonate with it. And so, but if they were on the webinar, it would resonate with them and they would buy the product. So I re-nitch out my front end page to kind of, to speak to different audiences if that makes sense. So awesome, of course, you can application every one of them are live. So I'll show you some of the other variations of the pages here in a second. All right, now I'm gonna go through this. So here's my first page here that I'm gonna register. So I'll register here and you'll see now it takes me to page number two in the funnel. Hey, this is rough. After they register, then it'll end on this page. And on this page, this is where, you know, my job's not done yet. I caused some curiosity, but now I gotta like drive it home and get them to actually show up. So a couple of things I do here. First thing I got a video from me saying, hi, thank you so much for registering. I'm talking to him like, this webinar class is gonna be amazing. This is what's called again, this is what you're gonna learn. It's re-getting and fired up and excited for that webinar, right? That's the first thing I do on this page here. Next thing I do is for me, I like to have people, first of all, write down the date and the time and the events, they don't forget. You have the date and time here. I added this because some people like, they register and go to webinar and then send the email for some reason. They're like, I need the email again. So I added it on this page. It's like, if you're doing a webinar, you can re-register here. This takes them just directly to the go to webinar page. So it's just kind of like a second backup. It's like when I went skydiving, the dude that was hooked to my back, he had two parachutes. And so I'm kind of a big believer in that and just like throwing out a second parachute just in case. So that's my second parachute for some reason that they didn't get to go to webinar link, right? Then over here now is I want them to do stuff before they show up at the event. There's a couple different schools of thought on this. One of them is, and this is like Frank Kern, he always sells something on his thank you page to try to cover his ad cost for the webinar, right? Because if I spend, I think on average, we're spending between $3 and $5 for someone to register for our webinar on Facebook. So I want to make sure that I recoup those costs before the webinar. So I usually on the thank you page, I'm selling something. So for me, what I'm selling is I give people a free two week trial to click funnels. So my video says that, hey thanks so much for registering for the webinar. Now before you get on the webinar, I'm gonna be talking to you about click funnels. You need to go get a trial to click funnels. If not, you're more on, I don't wanna say that, but. Go get a trial, click on the button down below and go get a free trial. So they click on that, they go get their free trial and then it starts putting them into that process. They start using the software. So now when they show up for the webinar, they're predisposed to it, they understand it, they're more likely to buy it, okay? The other concept again is like, let's say you're selling whatever, you're selling a $1,000 video course on the webinar. You might wanna do like, hey, I've got a $27 video quick start guide, go buy that right now. It's a quick start, I hope you get started into this whole video marketing, whatever, right? So I'm selling something to kind of help cover the lead cost, but secondly is I wanna get people in the process of buying with me, okay? Human beings are really interesting characters. Like when we start, when we're in motion, it's hard to stop us. When we're not in motion, it's hard to get us started, right? And so I wanna get the process started. I would rather somebody have bought something from me before I pitch them $1,000 things than not. So I'm gonna try to do that here on this page. So for me, I'm selling the free trial. So they're not buying anything but putting their credit card in before the webinar, right? And then I started doing this down here just to, again, make more money on our lead costs. And I have step number one, here's the date, times of the webinar. Step number two, go get your free trial. And then step number three, go buy my book. Okay, so now I'm kinda selling two things here, but they're just kind of like, each of these are really simple offers. Free trial, free book, they put their credit card, but now like I'm making money before the webinar ever starts. Does that make sense? So that's kind of what I'm doing on my registration page. A lot of different things. The core things, I'm re-getting them excited about the webinar, making sure they write down dates and time and trying to sell them something. So that's what I do on the thank you page. All right, this is called the death of a website. And so this is when I target when we're sending chiropractors and doctors and dentists and professionals, people who don't know what a funnel is, but they know what a website is. And this one converts awesome. I have a sales letter version of the registration page. I also have a video version that, again, normally I can find like three seconds, but it's just a little bit longer and it's kind of explaining them like the death of a website, right? So I find something that, something that, again, the webinar doesn't have to change because I'm still teaching the same thing, but it's a different hook that'll get that audience engaged. Because you'll find like, if you start, you know, you're building a webinar on Facebook traffic. And let's say after a while, like you're gonna run out of people to show it to you. Like you're gonna show it to everyone in that market, right? After usually a couple of months, we have what we call ad fatigue, where people seem to offer so many times that it stops responding as well. And you go from two or three dollars to registration like $10 or $15 and it gets more expensive. So for me, it's all about, I can re-nitch the front end and then we can target a different audience. So this was one version we had targeted and I was trying to show you a bunch of them, but anyway, conceptually you can kind of see how that all works. All right. Cool. Pull my other screen over here. Because next thing I was gonna show you the other registration or the pages. Let's see if I can find them real quick. Well, let's talk about conceptually and then I'll find the pages for you later. So here's conceptually now what we do. We call this the perfect webinar funnel. People always ask me, what day is the best day to a webinar and how long I should I start promoting it? What should I, blah, blah, blah, all these things like that. So I'll kind of walk you through what we do. And this isn't a hundred percent like the same for everything, right? Like if this, you know, this a lot of these things are happening if I have somebody who's sending emails from both the webinar. If I'm new to this on Facebook, this is gonna change a little bit, but not dramatically. So just kind of keep that in mind that like this process will tweak based on the type of traffic you're sending to it. But I'm gonna kind of explain it as if I have a joint venture partner who's promoting this webinar and then after I'll kind of tweak and show you how we do it if it's Facebook or some other type of ad source. So again, I call this the perfect webinar funnel. So in my perfect world, I like doing my webinars on Wednesday and then again on Friday. Okay, so I'm doing two webinars each week. Okay, and the reason is a lot of people I know it was funny when a bunch of students who were doing webinars once, they're doing like every Thursday or whatever. And so the webinar ended on Thursday and Friday start buying ads again. They drive ads Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday. And the show breaks were horrible. And the first they're all freaking out. It's like, oh, nobody's showing up. But we started digging deeper in the analytics. What we found that was interesting is that there's show up rates of people that registered Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday were really low, but people registered Wednesday and Thursday were awesome. Like they were all showing up. It was just because like most of the people, again, their attention spans are so short that they don't remember that far out. And so we had to compress that time. So for me, I like about a two and a half day lead time to a webinar. If I go, maybe three and a half days max, but not any longer than that. So that's why I like Wednesdays. I like to start my promotion on Monday, do a Monday promotion, Tuesday, Wednesday, Wednesday, then do a Wednesday webinar, and then kind of through there, okay? Cause I don't want more than two and a half days from when the first could hear about it to when the webinar happens. If it goes further than that, the show up rate just starts taking, okay? All right, so this idea, so Monday, I would have a joint venture partner promote my webinar registration page, okay? And then percentage of people will hit the confirmation page and then they're gonna hopefully buy something, right? So you guys saw that process already. Then after they register for the webinar, I send them what we call an indoctrination process or some indoctrination pages. These are pages to get them excited about the webinar, okay? Now in a perfect world, what would happen is someone register on Monday, then Monday they watch video number one, Tuesday they watch video number two, Wednesday morning they watch video number three, and then they show up on the webinar, okay? And so many people stress that about that, like Russell, they register on a Tuesday and then the webinar happened and then they got the other videos later and it's all messed up and I keep trying to stress, like it doesn't really matter, right? These indoctrination pages are amplifiers. They're not necessary to the sale. They're there to help strengthen the sale. So a lot of times people will register, they'll watch video number one, then they get on the webinar, then they watch video two, then video three, and then they buy after video three, right? It doesn't matter kind of where these hit, it's just having them there because it intensifies their desire and their likelihood to purchase when they're actually on the webinar, okay? Does that make sense? So it just don't stress out about like the timing, the sequencing, making sure it's perfect because it won't ever be, so don't worry about it, okay? So they register on Monday, boom, and then they get, start getting each day, they get video number one, video number two, video number three, and somewhere in that time they're watching the webinar and I'm trying to sell them, okay? So we push really hard for this live Wednesday webinar. So Monday mail, Tuesday mail, Wednesday we mail in the morning and then Wednesday we mail an hour before the webinar actually happens again, okay? A couple other tricks, I know Joel knows these, a couple other things we do, if you're using GoToWebinar, a cool trick you can do is GoToWebinar will send a message out an hour before the webinar saying don't forget your webinar starts in an hour. So what we do is 10 to 15 minutes before the webinar starts, I log in to GoToWebinar and then I change the title of the webinar from Russell's Cool webinar to Astrick Astrick starting now on bold caps, Astrick Astrick, and then Russell's Cool webinar. So I change the title of it and it says, hey, do you wanna notify all participants of the change of title? I say yes, and then GoToWebinar emails everybody 10 to 15 minutes before the webinar with a big email and the subject line says starting now and that is one of the biggest things we use to get people to actually show up at the webinar, it's amazing. Couple other tricks is if you grab cell phone numbers and you text using an hour before the webinar and then again, I like 10 minutes before the webinar so using that works as well. Those are all things I'm doing to get people to show up at the webinar, right? And again, if I've used a lot of curiosity, if my indoctrination videos strengthen the webinar and get people more excited and more interested showing up, it'll increase your show up rates. All those, I mean, there's a lot of pieces that go into that, but all those things together will help increase your show up rates, okay? You know, if your show up rates are under 20%, like you're in trouble, like either the market's horrible or you're not getting emails or it can be a lot of different things, but if it's sub 20%, it's gonna be hard to make the metrics work. Between 20% and like 40% is awesome. If you go above 40%, give me a call and let me know how you did it, because that's amazing, okay? So that's how we do it. So then the webinar happens on Wednesday. We do this one live. And I love doing webinars live and I wanna sell you on why you should do your webinars live first. One of the main reasons why you need to do it live, a whole bunch of times, not just once. Every time you do the webinar, you learn different things about why people are not buying. In fact, the very first time I did the ClickFunnels webinar, I was at an event in San Diego, speaking of Mike Phil's famous event, and I think I closed like 38% of the room. It was awesome. It was the first time I had done the webinar, stage presentation and it worked. And then the next morning I was flying out and I met this lady in the lobby and she's like, hey, your presentation was awesome and I wish I could have bought, but I don't have a supplement. Mike, what do you mean? She's like, well, I don't have a supplement and I can't really buy. I'm like, well, this isn't about supplements. This isn't about anything. I was just showing the supplement as an example. She's like, really? I'm like, what do you do? She's like, oh, I'm a coach. I'm like, this is made for coaches. She's like, really? Yeah, and so she's like, well, me and like three of my friends wanted to buy, but we don't have a supplement. But if we can buy, like we're gonna buy it. So they ran back and like ended up buying the next day when they found out that it worked for anyone, right? And I was like, it would always work for anyone, but she just didn't know that. But I never would have known that either if she didn't tell me that. So she told me that. And so what happened next, let me see if I can show you my slide. So after she told me that, I went back to my slides and checked this out. You guys will see how I transitioned this. So after I teach the very first thing, I show my supplement funnel. Then come right here, I say, because Jamel shares their supplement funnel in video. Okay, that's why they're like, oh, I wish I had a supplement funnel like his. So I came back, I had this slide, I said, like, well, who does this work for? Obviously it works for people's supplement funnels, but it also works for coaching funnels and info product funnels and e-commerce funnels and real business funnels. It works for all types of businesses. For example, here's my coaching funnel. How many of you guys are coaches or you wanna be a coach? And they're like half the audience. I'm like, cool, well, this is my coaching funnel. Makes me 70 grand a week. You should knock it off. How many of you guys sell information products? Here's my info product funnel. You should knock it off. Here's my e-commerce funnel. You should knock it off. Here's my chiropractors funnel. You should knock it off. So I'm showing them all these examples. And now if they're a chiropractor, they're like, oh, this does work for me. Or if they're an e-commerce, like, oh, it works for me. Okay, and then they also, they get it in all these other markets. And I didn't know that until she told me that. That she didn't understand it. So when she told me that, I tweaked that and increased my conversions, right? Then the next, we got back to an event. The next Tuesday I did a webinar. I had two webinars that day, one in the morning, one in the afternoon. We had more people registered for the morning webinar. I did the webinar and it did all right. We made like $30,000 in sales and I was kind of upset. And so afterwards I'm like, why aren't people buying? And I told my staff, I'm like, export all of the Q and A from go to webinar and I need to see why people aren't buying. And so I spent like 30 minutes reading every single question people had submitted. And all of a sudden it's like, wow. Everyone's having questions about this and about this and about this. And I found out all these questions. I went back in and I tweaked my slides. I changed a whole bunch of stuff. I moved things around. I changed the order. I shared case studies that would help strengthen positionings. I tried to answer all the questions people had. And then after I did that, then that is like four hours later I had the webinar. So I'm going fast like that and the webinar happens. I get on. We had less people on the webinar. I did the webinar and then that, when we closed it, we did $120,000 in sales on the live webinar. Four times as much from a couple of tweaks. When that webinar was over, I was like, holy crap, we made way more money. Hey, export all the questions. Let's find out what people are stuck at. Export the questions. I read them all, came back, tweaked my slides, tweaked my slides. Three days later with another presentation, boom, did 150 grand in sales. And export the questions. And I've done the webinar live probably, man, 40 or 50 times. Every time I export the questions, I change my slides. So this is the living, breathing thing that's always changing and always evolving. If I were to do one automated webinar and then just use that, I would have left millions and millions of dollars on the line. So there's a long way to tell you to do your webinars live the first time, right? Webinar live on Wednesday. When it's done, then I transition over and we say, to instead of going directly like, hey, there's another replay. Cause the problem is like, a lot of people get on the webinar and they're waiting for a replay. And so they never show up. And I will always make more money if they show up. Okay. And so I need them to show up. So what we do is we try to train them. And the webinar's over and say, hey, webinar was amazing. A ton of people are interested in it. So what we're gonna do is we're gonna do a live encore, I don't say live, so we're gonna do an encore webinar this Friday at the exact same time. You need to be on it. Okay. So then we promote that Thursday. Say, hey, Friday's webinars to have them go re-register. So re-register, re, get some of the videos. We're, it said to begin on Friday morning and then Friday hour before, hey, webinars happening. Now we got all these people re-engaged in the sequence, re-watching the videos. And now a huge percentage show up. They didn't show up the first time cause they're like, man, this is so good. I missed it. The encore is happening. So they came into the encore and then we sell them again. Now this time on the Friday webinar, I do not do those ones live. I take the recording from the earlier Wednesday webinar and then we replay that. And for us to use go to webinars, we replay it through go to webinar. And that's how we do the Friday webinar. Okay. There's people you can hire. There's a couple different companies that will actually, they'll log into your go to webinar account, they'll record your Wednesday webinar and they'll go and they'll push play on a video and it looks like it's live. People have no idea that it's not live cause it looks live. And I have my entire staff logged in to go to webinar answering Q and A during the webinar. And so it makes it seem very, very live. And usually we'll get not quite as many but almost as many sales from the Friday webinar as we do the Wednesday webinar. Then when Friday one's over, now we transition into like our, into like a replay they can go watch online. So Saturday morning, they get an email for a replay. And Sunday morning, they get an email for the replay. Plus what we do is we print out the cliff notes of the webinar, which is basically we take screen shot of everyone of the slides and we have some texts that kind of explains, that kind of they can read through the webinar. And I've had a big PDFs, like a 70 page PDF. So I've been like, here's the replay, but if you're in a rush, print out the cliff notes right now and go read these because it offers going away to night and midnight, like you need to go, you know, here's the cliff notes, read really, really quick. And then Sunday, we do one more, one more email six hours before we end, we close the cart, which gives them the replay, gives them the cliff notes, plus gives them urgency and scarcity. Now I don't know what it is about humans, but if we don't have urgency and scarcity, half the times we won't ever move. We get about a third of our sales in the last six hours, every single time. And it comes to me saying six hours left and it's all for disappears forever. If you want to click phones for free for a year, you've got six hours go. And people all of a sudden are gonna make decisions when something's about to disappear. And that right there is a sequence we take them through. So that's what I call the perfect webinar funnel. Now again, this is based on a joint venture partner emailing their list or S email list, right? They're sending one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, 10 emails, which may seem like a lot. And some people will never send you 10 emails. Some people will. So we try to get that. And they're like, I'm not gonna do 10 emails. I'm like, okay, how about you do Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and then let's do a Thursday and whatever it is, right? I get as many as I can on them because the more they get, the more money they'll make, the more money we'll make. Oh, I just found out about click funnels. And a lot of times it's funny, they don't even remember that like, I'm the one that told them to sign up, but they're like, oh, I heard about that somewhere the other day. It's like, yeah. The emails I sent out to you about talking about click funnels, which is kind of fun. Okay, and one more metric I wanna share with you guys on this, to kind of hopefully drive this whole thing home. So when we launched this webinar initially, and again, we've gone from, you know, click funnels, we first started trying to sell click funnels. We did a big product launch around it and it completely bombed. We had, I mean, way less science than I thought. I was kind of embarrassed and so forth. And about two months, I was just like depressed. And we spent like millions of dollars building click funnels. Like, I know it's the greatest thing since sliced bread, but nobody's buying it. Like, what's wrong? And luckily, my friend, Mike Philstein was like, hey man, I want you to come to my event and sell click funnels. And I was like, I can't sell, like we have free trial and nobody's even buying it, man. Like, I don't get it. And he's like, I don't care. I've got you on the docket. People are planning and you speaking on click funnels, you gotta figure it out. And you have to sell something for a thousand bucks. And I was like, oh man. And I literally didn't even start my presentation until his event started. I was still in Boise, Idaho. His event started in San Diego and they were streaming live online. So I had it on one of my monitors. I'm watching this thing, right? And then, so I'm watching this, the webinar happened, or really a live event. So this is like Friday. So it's Friday, I started my presentation. I'm flying out Saturday morning and I speak Saturday afternoon. And so like, I literally, all I did, I started this process the same way you guys should start it. I opened up the perfect webinar script, opened my PowerPoints, I started filling the blanks. I'm literally watching this event happen live. I'm like filling in the PowerPoints here. Took me out a day to get this whole thing, like a hard day, because I, you know, I was working really hard to get all of them, put the whole thing together, jumped into play the next morning, flew to San Diego, got up on stage, gave the presentation, just followed my own template. Didn't even deviate, just followed it to a T. And we closed 38% of the room and it just worked. And I went through the process I kind of told you guys about and it's been less than a year since that. I think it's two months from when that webinar happens. And that webinar has taken click funnels from being this tiny little company to, we're doing over a million dollars a month right now. And soon it'll be about two million dollars a month. It's just growing really, really rapidly. And it all came off this webinar, 100%. Now what happens is, a percentage of people will come into this funnel. They watch the webinar and then they buy. But without every single person who buys live on the webinar, three people sign up for the trial. And so while, you know, not everybody buys on the webinar, a huge percentage are joining our continuity program and now getting, now paying us monthly to be in there. And so it's just grown the residuals of our business as well. And so if you look at that as a whole, and I want to, I'm not telling this to brag, I'm telling you guys to inspire you that, that like one webinar can change your whole, can change your life.
